sources.notes: Statement and constraints: given desired prefix gcds and suffix gcds, decide whether an array can realize both.
Official editorial/code confirms constructing candidate a_i=lcm(prefix_i,suffix_i) and validating gcds.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate checked by search/open attempt; not used as a consulted solution source.

annotation.summary: Given arrays p and s, decide if there exists an array whose prefix gcd array is p and suffix gcd array is s.
annotation.constraints: Sum n up to 2e5 style; values fit 64-bit lcm checks; linear validation is intended.
annotation.core_idea: Any feasible a_i must be divisible by both p_i and s_i, so lcm(p_i,s_i) is the minimal canonical candidate. If any solution exists, replacing every a_i by this lcm candidate preserves the required divisibility constraints enough to test feasibility. Recompute prefix and suffix gcds of the candidate and compare with p and s.

solution_variants.name: lcm-candidate-validation
solution_variants.summary: Set a_i=lcm(p_i,s_i), then check all prefix and suffix gcds match the input arrays.

tags.evidence: The problem is exactly about realizing and validating prefix/suffix gcd arrays.
The lcm candidate follows from every element needing to be divisible by both its prefix and suffix gcd requirements.
The decision is made by constructing a canonical candidate array and checking it.
